name: new-ip-rtl
description: New IP RTL from scratch: MAS → ports → FSM → datapath → registers → interrupt → memory → lint

tasks.content: [FROM MAS] Read MAS handoff + extract all RTL requirements
tasks.detail:
This task is initiated from a [MAS HANDOFF] from mas_gen.

STEP 1 — Find the MAS file. Look for: <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md. If ip_name is not known yet, scan the current directory for */mas/*_mas.md. Confirm exactly one MAS file exists and is complete (has §1–§9).

STEP 2 — Extract and note all RTL requirements from each section:
- §2: port list (name / width / direction / clock-domain), parameters
- §3: FSM states + transitions + outputs, datapath operations per feature
- §4: register map (offset / name / width / access-type / reset-value)
- §5: interrupt sources + enable + status + W1C clear logic
- §6: memory instances (type / depth / width / latency)
- §7: timing constraints, CDC crossings
- §8: RTL coding style (reset polarity, nonblocking rules, lint waivers)

STEP 3 — Create RTL file skeleton: <ip_name>/rtl/<ip_name>.sv (empty module stub only). Do NOT implement anything yet.

Gate: MAS is fully read, all sections extracted, RTL file created.

tasks.content: Write module header: `default_nettype none, parameters, port declarations
tasks.detail: First lines: `default_nettype none. module <name> #(parameters) (ports); Match §2 port table exactly — every port with explicit direction+type+width. Use 'logic' for all signals. Add inline comments for clock domains on each port.

Declare state type enum. always_ff for state register with rst_n. always_comb for next-state logic (exhaustive case, default clause). always_comb for FSM output assignments (default at top). Every state/transition from MAS §3 FSM table must be present. No unreachable states.

tasks.content: Implement datapath always_ff blocks (MAS §3 Feature Operation)
tasks.detail: For each feature in MAS §3: implement the data registers as always_ff blocks. Nonblocking (<=). Active reset (rst_n or rst — per §8). Group by pipeline stage if §7 specifies pipeline depth.

tasks.content: Implement register map / CSR decode (MAS §4 FAM)
tasks.detail: APB/AXI address decode always_comb. Per-register always_ff: RW → written on write strobe; RO → driven by internal signal; RW1C → cleared by write-1. Read mux: always_comb with address select. Match MAS §4 offsets exactly.

For each interrupt source in MAS §5: implement level detection or edge capture. irq output = OR of (INT_STATUS[i] & INT_ENABLE[i]). W1C clear: INT_STATUS[i] <= INT_STATUS[i] & ~wdata[i] on write. Verify irq deasserts within 1 cycle of W1C clear.
Instantiate each memory from MAS §6. Match: depth, width, port count, latency. Add read-enable, write-enable, address, data ports per §2 interface. Verify timing matches §7 (read latency cycles).

tasks.content: Write filelist and lint check: 0 errors, 0 warnings
tasks.detail: Write <ip_name>/list/<ip_name>.f — one line per RTL file path (e.g. <ip_name>/rtl/<ip_name>.sv). Run /lint <ip_name>/rtl/<ip_name>.sv. Fix all errors. Width mismatch warnings: fix or add //lint_waive with comment. Undriven output warnings: add explicit '0 assignment. Write <ip_name>/lint/lint_report.txt. Report to mas_gen: [MAS RESULT] rtl_gen DONE.
